EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Just two days after one storm system produced heavy snow in the higher elevations of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin, a second storm came through on a track slightly south of the first one. This storm produced heavy snow down to the desert floor over much of the Mojave Desert.  Snow was seen as low as 1500 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell over much of the southeast half of the Las Vegas Valley.  The highest total was 8 inches, measured on the south side of Henderson. Many cars slid off roads, and others were abandoned at the bottoms of hills.  At an apartment complex, a large carport collapsed under the weight of the snow, damaging several vehicles parked underneath.  Many trees were bent down to the ground, or snapped in two due to the weight of the snow, blocking roads.  The Clark County School District closed schools due to snow for the first time in 30 years.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought over two feet of snow to parts of the Sierra Nevada and a rare white Christmas to much of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts to 82 knots (94 mph) were recorded on the summit of Virginia Peak (elev. 8299 ft), the location of the NWS Reno WSR-88D weather radar which was damaged by strong winds on December 19th.  The wind gusted to 94 mph during a one-and-a-half-hour period from approximately 215 am PST to 330 am PST Christmas morning.  These strong winds further damaged the radome and the now-exposed radar transmitter.  Most of the fiberglass radome was torn from the radar pedestal and fell to the ground.  Exposed to the force of the wind, the radar transmitter was damaged beyond repair.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system moved through the northern Sierra and western Nevada and brought snow and high winds to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong synoptic-scale winds moving across the Sierra Nevada spread into western Nevada the evening of December 18th.  The strongest wind gusts occurred after midnight and through the morning hours of the 19th.  From approximately 230 am PST and 245 am PST wind gusts to 123 knots (142 mph) were recorded at the summit of Virginia Peak (elev. 8299 ft).  The NWS Reno WSR-88D weather radar sits atop Virginia Peak.  The radome (which protects the radar transmitter from the elements) was buckled inward by the strong wind gusts.  This caused the radar transmitter to stop rotating, thus shutting down the radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture brought severe thunderstorms and flash flooding to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Near downtown Las Vegas, lightning struck a tree, which then fell into power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported 3/4 inch hail on U.S. Highway 95 between Yerington and Silver Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported nickel-sized hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 3 miles east of Lahontan Reservoir reported a 58-knot (67-mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter in Silver Springs estimated wind gusts of up to 70 knots (80 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported a wall cloud and a funnel cloud over Lahontan Reservoir.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 1 mile southwest of Fallon estimated wind gusts of 52 knots (60 mph).  Power outages were reported along with areas of blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 4 miles north of Fallon reported nickel-sized hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system approaching the area brought high winds to central and northern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Morey Creek RAWS site had a wind gust to 75 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought early season snowfall to the east-central Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mount Rose ski area reported 8.0 inches of snowfall since Monday afternoon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Snow-covered ground contributed to the formation of freezing fog across portions of Churchill and Pershing Counties in western Nevada.  The fog dissipated with the addition of surface heating during the morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Freezing fog, reducing visibilities to less than one-quarter mile at times, stretched across southern Pershing and northern Churchill Counties overnight.  The main areas affected were along Interstate 80 from just east of Fernley to the Rye Patch Reservoir, and along U. S. Highway 95 between Fallon and Trinity Junction.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A complex winter storm brought high winds and snow to northern and central Nevada. The high winds caused blowing and drifting of the snow which reduced visibilities to less than 1/4 mile at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and snow to Humboldt county. Frequent wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph occurred with this storm with occasional gusts of 45 to 55 mph. This reduced visibility to less than 1/4 mile at times in snow and blowing snow. A peak gust of 67 mph was recorded at the Morey Creek RAWS station. Two inches of new snow fell at Winnemucca and McDermitt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving snowstorm dropped up to 2 feet of snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 8 miles south of Reno reported a 56 knot (65 mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada from January 4th through the 6th.  Up to 10 feet of snow fell in the higher elevations of the Sierra with 1 to 2 feet in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 40-foot-long break in the Truckee Canal released floodwater into portions of Fernley, Nevada on the morning of January 5th.  Local newspapers reported that approximately 1500 people had to be evacuated from their homes.  Water 2 to 4 feet deep affected as many as 500 homes.  The floodwaters started to recede the afternoon of January 5th but remained over the area until the afternoon of January 6th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada from January 4th through the 6th.  Up to 10 feet of snow fell in the higher elevations of the Sierra with 1 to 2 feet in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 40-foot-long break in the Truckee Canal released floodwater into portions of Fernley, Nevada on the morning of January 5th.  Local newspapers reported that approximately 1500 people had to be evacuated from their homes.  Water 2 to 4 feet deep affected as many as 500 homes.  The Reno Gazette-Journal newspaper reported that more than $1.75 million in federal disaster housing assistance had been provided.  In addition to this, Fernley city officials estimated that the cost to repair roads would be over $720,000.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought strong winds to the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 51 knot (59 mph) wind gust was recorded at the Hawthorne airport.  Also, a trained weather spotter reported Highway 95 North closed due to high winds and blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought strong winds to the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The following wind gusts were recorded:||Nevada DOT wind sensor in Washoe Valley: 56 knots (64 mph);  |Galena RAWS sensor (elev. 5610 ft): 51 knots (59 mph); |NWS Forecast Office in north Reno: 50 knots (58 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought heavy snowfall to the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mt. Rose ski area reported 23 inches of snowfall in the past 48 hours.||A trained weather spotter located 1 mile northeast of Incline Village reported 13.0 inches of snowfall in the past 48 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought heavy snowfall to the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off-duty NWS employee reported 5.0 inches of snowfall in the northwest Reno foothills at an elevation of 5000 feet.||A resident of southwest Reno reported a 12-hour total of 7.0 inches of snowfall at an elevation of 4850 feet.||A trained weather spotter located 2 miles south of Spanish Springs in northeast Sparks reported a 12-hour total of 5.8 inches of snowfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system approaching the area brought high winds to central and northern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A weather spotter in Baker reported a wind gust to 82 mph and the Ely ASOS |reported a wind gust to 74 mph. The high winds partially blew off a roof from the Baker Elementary School and caused a power outage in the town of Baker.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful snowstorm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada on the 23rd and 24th.  Up to 3 feet of snow fell in the Sierra with up to a foot in portions of western Nevada.  Strong winds also accompanied the cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 50 knot (58 mph) wind gust was recorded by the Nevada DOT sensor near Walker Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of powerful Pacific storms brought heavy precipitation and strong winds to southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer recorded 10 inches of snow in Kyle Canyon (7600').

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of powerful Pacific storms brought heavy precipitation and strong winds to southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Localized downslope winds off the Spring Mountains caused damage 10 miles northwest of downtown Las Vegas.  Ten homes lost roof tiles; nine of the ten suffered only minor damage.  The initial report was winds estimated at 100 mph; however, a NWS meteorologist surveyed the damage and interviewed several homeowners later the same day, and the consensus was gusts near 65 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong cold front brought widespread high winds and damage to portions of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The strongest measured gust occurred at McCarran International Airport (KLAS) at 1900 PST.  The airport was closed for a time so that windblown debris could be removed from the runways.  A Port-A-Potty blew across the main runway, damaging runway lighting and a parked vehicle.  Elsewhere in the valley, a mobile home was blown into a neighboring house, and a large tree crushed a truck.  The North Las Vegas Department of Public Works reported 5 street light poles, 8 stop signs, and 3 traffic lights blown down.  Many street vendors who were selling Valentine's Day gifts had much of their inventory blown away.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 55 knot (63 mph) wind gust was recorded at the Washoe Valley NDOT station 3 miles south of Washoe City.  Blowing snow was reducing visibility to less than 1/4 mile at times.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 56 knot (65 mph) wind gust was recorded at the National Weather Service Forecast Office in north Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 54 knot (62 mph) wind gust was recorded by the wind sensor at Wolf Run Golf Course in south Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Diamond Peak Ski Resort, 12 inches;|Mt. Rose ski area, 12 inches|one mile northeast of Incline Village, 5 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 50 knot (58 mph) wind gust was recorded by the Black Springs NDOT station in Golden Valley 7 miles north of Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of Pacific storms brought high winds and locally heavy snow to portions of southeast California and southern Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 13 miles NNW of Lathrop Wells.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Highway Patrol reported that an empty tractor-trailer was blown over by strong winds on U.S. Highway 395 near the Red Rock exit 10 miles northwest of Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Reno Gazette-Journal reported that two power lines were down and arcing on Moana Lane, forcing the rerouting of traffic.  Approximately 600 customers in the area were without power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A snowstorm at the end of January and the beginning of February brought strong winds and over a foot of snow to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off-duty NWS employee located in southwest Reno reported a 65 knot (75 mph) wind gust at an elevation of 4880 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought up to 3 feet of snow to the Sierra Nevada with a foot or more of snow to portions of western Nevada.  Strong winds affected the region before cold front moved through the night of February 2nd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 3 miles south of Reno reported a 50 knot (58 mph) wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought heavy snow to the Ruby Mountains and the East Humboldt Range. Snotel sites generally measured between 8-14 inches of new snow. The Hole-In-Mountain Snotel received 24 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving snowstorm dropped up to 2 feet of snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 5 miles WSW of Gardnerville reported a 53 knot (61 mph) wind gust.  The strong wind blew an antenna off of his roof.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving snowstorm dropped up to 2 feet of snowfall in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Diamond Peak Ski Resort reported a total of 8 inches of snowfall overnight.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through the northern and central Sierra Nevada and western Nevada on April 14th and 15th. Slight damage occurred throughout the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Reno Gazette-Journal newspaper reported wind damage which occurred across northwest Reno during the afternoon of April 14th.  A portion of a roof was blown off of a house on Tamra Drive.  A tree was blown over onto a house on Yori Avenue.  And a carport was blown into a house on Thoma Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought over two feet of snow to parts of the Sierra Nevada and a rare white Christmas to much of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The following 12-hour storm total snowfall amounts were reported:||A spotter in Lovelock reported 4 inches.|A spotter 12 miles south of Winnemucca reported 8 to 10 inches.||In addition to snowfall, the Nevada Highway Patrol noted several accidents involving automobiles sliding off Interstate 80.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A complex winter storm brought high winds and snow to northern and central Nevada. The high winds caused blowing and drifting of the snow which reduced visibilities to less than 1/4 mile at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought strong winds and snow to White Pine county. Frequent wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph occurred with this storm with occasional gusts of 40 to 50 mph. This reduced visibility to less than 1/4 mile at times in snow and blowing snow. A weather spotter in Ely reported widespread snowfall of 4 to 6 inches across town with localized amounts of 8 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought strong winds and heavy snow to northern Nevada. Three to seven inches of new snow was reported across northern Nevada. In addition strong winds with the storm caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow. Snow drifts up to four feet were reported in Spring Creek and Ryndon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snotel sites reported 8 to 12 inches of new snow. In addition,strong winds caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to northern Elko County. Tuscarora and Wild Horse Reservoir picked up 6 inches of new snow. In addition, winds of 25 to 35 mph with gusts to 45 mph caused considerable |blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to southwest and south central Elko County. Elko and Oasis received 7 inches of new snow and Ryndon picked up 8 inches. In addition, winds of 20 to 30 mph with gusts to 40 mph caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow. Spring creek had drifts as deep as 4 feet across roads and driveways.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought strong winds and heavy snow to northern Nevada. Three to seven inches of new snow was reported across northern Nevada. In addition strong winds with the storm caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow. Snow drifts up to four feet were reported in Spring Creek and Ryndon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twelve inches of new snow was reported in Charleston. In addition, winds of 30 to 40 mph with gusts to 50 mph caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought strong winds and heavy snow to northern Nevada. Three to seven inches of new snow was reported across northern Nevada. In addition strong winds with the storm caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow. Snow drifts up to four feet were reported in Spring Creek and Ryndon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four to seven inches of new snow was reported. In addition, winds of 25 to 35 mph with gusts to 45 mph caused considerable blowing and drifting snow creating drifts up to 4 feet in Spring Creek and Ryndon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to portions of northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought heavy snow and strong winds to southern Lander and southern Eureka counties. A weather spotter in Austin reported 6 inches of new snow. In addition, winds of 20 to 30 mph with gusts to 45 mph caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through the northern Sierra and northwestern Nevada on March 26th.  Strong winds were experienced ahead of, and with, the cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Appeal newspaper in Carson City reported that 50-mph wind gusts blew over two 40-foot-tall cottonwood trees in Carson City during the afternoon of March 26th. Only minor damage to the roof of a house was reported. The newspaper said that it was discovered that the bulk of the trees missed the structure.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moving through northeast California and northwest Nevada produced strong gusty winds across the region.  Areas of blowing dust caused visibility problems in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The wind sensor located 2 miles south of Washoe City in Washoe Valley recorded a wind gust of 54 knots (62 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong low pressure system moving through the Pacific Northwest brought high winds to portions of southeast California and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated winds gusting over 60 mph in the town of Dyer.  Visibility was near zero, with rocks blowing through the air.  The wind and rocks damaged the roof of the spotter's house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms brought locally heavy rainfall and hail to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lahontan Valley News reported that a woman lost control of her vehicle on slick pavement due to an accumulation of small hail.  She was ejected from her SUV as it rolled down an embankment.  The accident occurred on U.S. Highway 95 near Lyon County Road 2.  According to the newspaper she was pronounced dead at the scene of the accident by the Lyon County coroner.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong low pressure system and cold front brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 62 miles N of Beatty, NV.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong low pressure system and cold front brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 9 miles NW of Mercury, NV.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front passed through the region on May 20th.  Strong winds were experienced in wind prone areas ahead of the front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada DOT wind sensor at Walker Lake recorded a wind gust of 50 knots (58 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low level instability combined with an upper level jet max to produce scattered strong thunderstorms across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter estimated a wind gust of 52 knots (60 mph) at the Pyramid Lake boat ramp.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low level instability combined with an upper level jet max to produce scattered strong thunderstorms across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 51 knots (59 mph) was recorded at the Dead Camel RAWS site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong low level instability combined with an upper level jet max to produce scattered strong thunderstorms across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 52 knots (60 mph) was recorded by the DRI sensor at Rawhide Flats.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A push of monsoon moisture from the southeast brought severe storms and flash flooding to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water, mud, debris and boulders were crossing U.S. Highway 95 at Esmeralda County mile marker 7.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A push of monsoon moisture from the southeast brought severe storms and flash flooding to portions of the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and knocked down power lines in Pahrump.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a microburst in Ely with a wind gust of 61 mph at the Ely airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A microburst produced a wind gust to 61 mph at the Ely airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused minor damage in Cresent Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down a large tree onto a fence and pickup truck. A shed was blow over and some minor roof damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds cause minor damage in Ryndon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused some minor roof and tree damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture triggered a thunderstorm which brought isolated flooding to Lincoln County, Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 322 was washed out between mile markers 9 and 14.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought up to 3 feet of snow to the Sierra Nevada with a foot or more of snow to portions of western Nevada.  Strong winds affected the region before cold front moved through the night of February 2nd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought up to 3 feet of snow to the Sierra Nevada with a foot or more of snow to portions of western Nevada.  Strong winds affected the region before cold front moved through the night of February 2nd.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Mt. Rose ski area, 27 inches;|1 mile NE of Incline Village, 19 inches (at 6700 feet);|Diamond Peak Ski Resort, 12 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada from January 4th through the 6th.  Up to 10 feet of snow fell in the higher elevations of the Sierra with 1 to 2 feet in western Nevada.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm brought 2 to 3 feet of snow to the Sierra Nevada, and strong winds to the Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Diamond Peak Ski Resort, 24 inches;|Daggett Pass, 14 inches of snowfall, with 4 to 5 foot snow drifts;|1 mile NE of Incline Village (at 6700 feet), 12 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of virga with embedded light rain showers moved over southeast California on the afternoon of the 29th and southern Nevada on the morning of the 30th, producing isolated high winds due to evaporational cooling in the dry subcloud layer.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at the North Las Vegas airport (KVGT).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported a funnel cloud west of Fallon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located in Silver Springs reported one-and-a-quarter-inch-diameter hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong supercell thunderstorms produced tornadoes, large hail, and heavy rainfall across western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 1 mile southwest of Fallon reported one-and-a-quarter-inch-diameter hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada from January 4th through the 6th.  Up to 10 feet of snow fell in the higher elevations of the Sierra with 1 to 2 feet in western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Virginia City, 23 inches (at 6340 feet);|northwest Reno, 20 inches (at 5350 feet);|Stead, 18 inches (at 5120 feet);|Carson City, 15 inches;|Golden Valley (5 miles north of Reno), 15 inches;|Cold Springs Valley (north of Reno), 14 inches of snowfall (with total water equivalent of 3.44 inches);|3 miles north of Spanish Springs (northeast of Reno), 12 inches;|Minden, 12 inches (at 4709 feet);|1 mile north of Carson City, 11 inches;|west Reno, 9 inches;|Spanish Springs (northeast of Reno), 9 inches (at 4488 feet).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture led to thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, some of which produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In the town of Mountain Springs, three feet of water flooded one business, one house was flooded, and a road was damaged.  In addition, Highway 160 was closed at Highway 159 due to water, mud and a boulder on the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture led to thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, some of which produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding affected much of East Las Vegas and the Green Valley section of Henderson.  At least five swift water rescues occurred, and many roads were covered by floodwaters which ranged up to two feet deep.  At least one building - a convenience store - was also flooded.  Two gages in the Green Valley section of Henderson measured between 1.10 and 1.30 inches of rain in 30 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture led to thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, some of which produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several large trees were blown down in the Green Valley section of Henderson.  In addition, many smaller tree limbs were broken off, and pea size hail fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture led to thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, some of which produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A mother and her 4-year old son were injured by a lightning strike in front of their home.  The boy was knocked unconscious, and the mother was temporarily paralyzed on one side of her body.  Both have fully recovered.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another push of monsoon moisture led to thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, some of which produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three inches of water flooded a resort building.  The water receded quickly.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful snowstorm moved through the Sierra Nevada and western Nevada on the 23rd and 24th.  Up to 3 feet of snow fell in the Sierra with up to a foot in portions of western Nevada.  Strong winds also accompanied the cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Heavenly Ski Resort, 18 inches;|3 miles WNW of Carson City, 12 inches (at 5280 feet);|1 mile NNW of Carson City, 10.5 inches;|3 miles NNE of Topaz Lake, 10 inches;|10 miles south of Reno, 9 inches;|Caughlin Ranch area of southwest Reno, 9 inches;|5 miles east of Gardnerville, 9 inches;|1 mile NE of Carson City, 8.5 inches, with a 0.58-inch water equivalent;|south Reno, 7 inches (at 4500 feet);|NWS Forecast Office in north Reno, 7 inches;|3 miles northwest of Carson City, 7 inches, with occasional snowfall rates of between 1.0 and 1.5 inches per hour;|1 mile NNW of Carson City, 7 inches;|Dayton, 7 inches;|Golden Valley area 5 miles north of Reno, 6 inches;|3 miles NNE of Minden, 6 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter-like storm brought heavy snow to the Ruby Mountains and the East Humboldt Range. Most Snotel sites reported between 7 and 13 inches of new snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter-like storm brought between 7 and 13 inches of snow to the Ruby Mountains and the East Humboldt Range.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High temperatures contributed to the deaths of two people in the Las Vegas area.  One death occurred on the 1st, when the high temperature was 110 degrees, and temperatures had been 105 degrees or higher for 10 out of 11 days.  The other death occurred on the 4th, when the high temperature was 100 degrees, and temperatures had been 100 degrees or higher for 16 out of 18 days.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people in the Las Vegas area died of heat-related causes - one on the 1st and one on the 4th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winnemucca received 7.8 inches of snow. In addition wind gust to 45 mph caused blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought heavy snow to the area. Wildhorse reported 12 inches of new snow, Tuscarora received 9 inches, and Charleston picked up 6 inches. In addition strong gusty winds to 45 mph caused blowing and drifting of the snow with reports of 3 to 4 foot drifts in Wildhorse.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and blowing snow to the area. Three to four inches of new snow fell. In addition winds gusting to 55 mph reduced visibility to near zero at times and caused considerable drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm heavy snow and high winds to the area. Snotel sites reported 7 to 10 inches of snow. In addition strong winds caused blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four to five inches of new snow was reported in and around Ely. Winds gusting to 45 mph caused considerable blowing and drifting of the snow with visibilities reduced to less than 1/4 mile at times.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm brought snow and strong gusty winds to northern and central Nevada. Many locations picked up 3 to 6 inches of snow with a few locations receiving 8 to 12 inches of snow. In addition strong gusty winds caused blowing and drifting snow with near zero visibility at times.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three to four inches of snow was reported in Tonopah. Winds gusting to 50 mph caused blowing and drifting of the snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system which moved through the central Sierra Nevada and western Nevada brought strong winds, high elevation snow, and lake effect snowfall to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 3 miles WSW of Yerington reported a total of 3 inches of snowfall during the 4-hour period from 8 am through just before noon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High temperatures contributed to the deaths of two people in the Las Vegas area on September 4th.  The high temperature was 102 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people in the Las Vegas area died of heat-related causes.
